sections.paragraph:
ἐπαφρίζω (Moschus 5, 5 intr. ‘foam up’) trans.: to cause to splash up like froth, cause to foam τὶ fig. κύματα ἐπαφρίζοντα (ἀπαφρ. v.l.) τὰς ἑαυτῶν αἰσχύνας waves casting up their own shameless deeds like (dirty) foam, perh. imaging the dirty appearance of foam created under certain conditions Jd 13.—DELG s.v. ἀφρός.

Moschus Moschus , bucol., II b.c.—List 5

intr. intr. = intransitive

trans. trans. = transitive

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

perh. perh. = perhaps

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 360
